steps.title: Recognize the concavity of $f(x) = 2^x$
steps.content: The exponential function $f(x) = 2^x$ is concave up. This is confirmed by the second derivative: $$f''(x) = 2^x (\ln 2)^2 > 0$$, which is always positive.

steps.title: Determine the region where g(x) > f(x)
steps.content:
For a concave up function, any straight line through two points on the curve lies above the curve between those points.

Since $g(x)$ is a line passing through the intersection points $(h, j)$ and $(k, m)$ on $f(x) = 2^x$, we have $g(x) > f(x)$ for all $h < x < k$.

Therefore, $h < x < k$, which matches choice D — D is correct.
